scenario: Failure-recovery/short-circuit path — no active sprint exists (docs/sprints/next-sprint/ is missing), so sprint-kickoff stops at Step 1 before touching git at all.
preconditions: Target project's CLAUDE.md contains the Nexa Rules Gate marker
docs/sprints/next-sprint/ does not exist (no /sprint-prepare has been run yet, or the prior sprint was already completed and archived without starting a new one)
expected_steps: Step 1: docs/sprints/next-sprint/ checked and found missing
Pipeline stops immediately with: 'No active sprint found. Run /sprint-prepare to start a sprint.'
Step 2 (sprint numbering), Step 3 (summary), Step 4 (branch creation — no git status/fetch/checkout/push commands run), and Step 5 (confirmation/delegation) are never reached
expected_terminal_outcome: The 'No active sprint found' message is shown; no git branch is created or pushed; /sprint-deliver is never invoked.
pass_criteria: The pipeline exits at Step 1 without running any git commands (no accidental branch creation from main with no sprint scope); the message correctly directs the user to /sprint-prepare rather than attempting to recover automatically.
